theory.canonical_statement: The Gaussian window insertion operator localizes the bridge-carrier construction in time. Instead of acting globally on the signal, the bridge tone is inserted only inside a smooth envelope centered at \(t_0\), giving a finite event rather than an everywhere-on modulation.
theory.technical_interpretation: Define a time-localized Gaussian envelope g(t) = exp(-{[}(t -- t$_0$)²{]}/(2\,$\sigma$²)). A signal combining scaled carriers and the bridge tone is

latex_blocks: s_{\mathrm{mix}}(t)=f_A'\cos(2\pi f_A' t)+f_B'\cos(2\pi f_B' t)+f_{\mathrm{bridge}}\cos(2\pi f_{\mathrm{bridge}} t)
canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
g(t)=\exp\!\left(-\frac{(t-t_0)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)
\]
\[
s_{\mathrm{mix}}(t)=f_A'\cos(2\pi f_A' t)+f_B'\cos(2\pi f_B' t)+f_{\mathrm{bridge}}\cos(2\pi f_{\mathrm{bridge}} t)
\]
\[
\mathcal{G}[s](t)=g(t)\,s_{\mathrm{mix}}(t)
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This defines the smooth time-localization that makes the insertion finite.

formula_latex_blocks: s_{\mathrm{mix}}(t)=f_A'\cos(2\pi f_A' t)+f_B'\cos(2\pi f_B' t)+f_{\mathrm{bridge}}\cos(2\pi f_{\mathrm{bridge}} t)
equation_progression.interpretive_role: This collects the aligned carriers and the bridge tone into one pre-window signal.
This closes the progression by expressing the actual insertion as the product of envelope and bridge-bearing signal.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\paragraph{Primary Equation.}
\[
g(t)=\exp\!\left(-\frac{(t-t_0)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)
\]
\[
s_{\mathrm{mix}}(t)=f_A'\cos(2\pi f_A' t)+f_B'\cos(2\pi f_B' t)+f_{\mathrm{bridge}}\cos(2\pi f_{\mathrm{bridge}} t)
\]
\[
\mathcal{G}[s](t)=g(t)\,s_{\mathrm{mix}}(t)
\]

\paragraph{Primary Derivation.}
The identity \( g(t)=\exp\!\left(-\frac{(t-t_0)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right) \) is an operator relation. Applying the operator to the relevant function and using its algebraic properties (such as linearity, commutators, or derivative rules) leads to the displayed equality.
\paragraph{Equation Type.}
This statement is classified as a operator relation.

\paragraph{Interpretation.}
This relation applies to the quantities defined above. From the TRAWIN perspective, the equation functions as the operator-level carrier for the entry’s information content. In CHL terms, the derivation provides an explicit term connecting the canonical proposition to its proof certificate.
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
